title: wicked-installer install marker v2
description: The bookkeeping file each install script writes at <configDir>/wicked-installer/<cli>-install.json (one per config dir it writes into). A statement of what exists on disk, not of success — written on every non-dry run including partial/failed. files[] is the manifest that makes exact uninstall possible. v1 markers ({ installedAt, <cli>Home, products: [...] } with no markerVersion) remain valid input to status/uninstall forever and are NOT described by this schema. See INTERFACE.md §10.

products.description: Keyed by product id. Per-product merge (upsert by id), never whole-file overwrite; entries for products not in this run are preserved.

files.description: Every path/config key this script CREATED (post-sanitization destination names, never source paths). Appended as operations succeed (journal), so a half-failed product lists exactly its partial footprint. This manifest is what enables EXACT removal: on upgrade the stale-sweep deletes every entry absent from the new set, and on uninstall the recorded set is removed — each deletion ownership-gated (§8.5/§12.3). It is also the primary source for the REQUIRED stale-artifact purge (§12.4); prior-era artifacts a marker never recorded (legacy/v1 markers) fall back to the product-prefixed naming heuristic. No dedicated purge field is needed — dir/file records already name the exact paths to remove.

path.description: configDir-relative when inside the config dir, else absolute (~/ shorthand allowed); forward slashes always.

wroteHash.description: sha256: + hash of the canonical (sorted-key) JSON of the value written. Guards deletion: uninstall removes the key only if the current value still hashes to this.

prior.description: The pre-existing value, non-null only when --force replaced a foreign value; enables restore on uninstall.

ownerMatch.description:
Ownership derivable from the entry itself (survives marker loss): an entry is owned by product P iff its command contains wicked-installer/products/

(compare with normalized separators).
